title: Data Packing for PostgreSQL
text: Data Packing for PostgreSQL Uniface packing codes are mapped to PostgreSQL storage formats (data types). Search: Column Uniface Packing Code PostgreSQL Storage Format Filter results... 37 rows found Table 1. Uniface to PostgreSQL Storage Formats Uniface Packing Code PostgreSQL Storage Format Filter 37 rows found Filter 37 rows found Uniface Packing Code PostgreSQL Storage Format B1-B3 char B4-B8 boolean C1-C65536 char( n ) C* varchar D date D1-D16 date E timestamp without time zone E1-E16 timestamp without time zone F double precision F4 real F8 double precision I1-I2 smallint I3-I4 integer I8 bigint J1-J32 numeric M1-M8 numeric N numeric N1-N32 numeric O1-O32 numeric P1-P16 numeric Q1-Q8 numeric R1-R* bytea T, T1 time without time zone T1-T2 time without time zone T3 timestamp without time zone T4 time without time zone T5-T6 timestamp without time zone T9-T16 time without time zone U1-U65536 varchar U* varchar VC1-VC* varchar VR1-CR* bytea VU1-VU* varchar VW1-VW* varchar W Text Y1-Y32 Bytea Z1-Z8 Bytea PostgreSQL Storage Formats The following table is intended as a guide, not a complete discussion of PostgreSQL storage formats. If you are in any doubt about the exact requirements in your environment, see your PostGreSQL documentation for more information. Table 2. PostgreSQL Storage Formats Storage format Description boolean Use the boolean storage format for fields which store TRUE or FALSE state information. A NULL value represents the Unknown state. bytea Use bytea storage format fields for variable-length binary data, such as images and other raw data. bigint Use the bigint for whole numbers from -9223372036854775808 to +9223372036854775807. It is intended for use when the range of the integer type is insufficient. char( n ) character( n ) Values of type character are physically padded with spaces to the specified width n . character varying( n ) varchar( n ) Use varchar when a variable-length field is required. Use this storage format when the expected length of your data can vary considerably, in a name field for example, when the same field will be used to hold the values ‘JJB Inc.’ and ‘International Sub-Oceanic Refuse Engineering Corporation’. The storage size of a Varchar field depends on its contents, if the field is empty, no space is used. date Use the date storage type to store the date, without the time of day. double precision double precision is a storage format for floating point numbers. Positive and negative numbers are allowed, as are numbers with exponential components (which are useful for scientific notation). The storage size is eight bytes. numeric numeric is a variable length storage format for exact numbers. PostgreSQL supports values up to 131072 digits before the decimal point and up to 16383 digits after the decimal point. Uniface only supports this data type with a precision of up to N32. timestamp timestamp without time zone Use timestamp without time zone storage format to store year, month, day, hour, minute, and second values. integer Use the integer for whole numbers between -2147483648 to +2147483647. The storage size is 4 bytes. real This is a four-byte version of the double precision storage format. smallint Use the bigint for whole numbers from -32768 to +32767. It is intended for use when disk space is at a premium. time Use the time storage type to store the time of day, without the date. text Use the text storage format for character data with a variable unlimited length.
